error_signature: Bad Request: bad webhook: Failed to resolve host: Name or service not known
title: Telegram webhook fails to resolve host
symptom: When trying to use the Telegram node, the error 'Bad Request: bad webhook: Failed to resolve host: Name or service not known' is displayed. This prevents the webhook from functioning correctly.
applies_when: Using a placeholder domain in webhook URL
not_this_if: Using a valid, reachable domain for webhook
root_cause: The error occurs because the webhook URL is set to a placeholder domain (e.g., n8n.example.com) that is not a real, reachable domain. Telegram cannot resolve this domain, leading to the failure.
fix_steps: Open your docker-compose.yml file where n8n is configured.
Set the WEBHOOK_URL environment variable to your actual domain (e.g., WEBHOOK_URL=https://your.actual-domain.com).
Recreate your n8n container by running 'docker-compose down' followed by 'docker-compose up -d'.
agent_instruction: Update the WEBHOOK_URL in your Docker configuration to your actual domain and recreate the n8n container.
